title: F.L.U.D.D
description: Adds F.L.U.D.D the multifunction water pump to the game.
body:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/Ifo_ouGMa7Y" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

# Introducing F.L.U.D.D!
The F.L.U.D.D is a multifunction, portable water pump from _Super Mario Sunshine_. The water it shoots can be used to clean, destroy or extinguish various blocks around your world. F.L.U.D.D also comes with 3 separate nozzles that can spice up the way you travel around your world.

# Features
- F.L.U.D.D that let's you spray entities and blocks
- 3 secondary nozzles that let you hover, dash and launch yourself

## List of blocks and entities that interact with a F.L.U.D.D's spray
### Blocks
- Exposed, weathered and oxidized copper blocks (will clean)
- Blocks of concrete powder (will turn into concrete)
- Campfires, lava and fire (will extinguish)
- Short grass, tall grass and vines (will destroy)
- Sculk blocks (will destroy)
- Moss blocks (will destroy)
- Cobweb (will destroy)
- Snow (will destroy)

### Entities
- Blaze (will damage)
- Magma cube (will damage)
- Slime (will damage)
- Silverfish (will damage)
- Enderman (will damage)
- Vex (will damage)
- Bat (will damage)
- Sheep (will clean)
- Any fireball or snowball (will destroy)


# How to install/uninstall
Download "F.L.U.D.D (datapack)" and put the .zip file in the datapacks folder of the world you want to play in. Download "F.L.U.D.D (resourcepack)" and put the .zip file in your "resourcepack" folder. Do not forget to enable the resourcepack in the settings and you'll be ready to go! To uninstall, enter the command "/function fludd:debug/uninstall" and remove the datapack from the datapacks folder or your world.

# How to get started
Here's the crafting recipe to make a F.L.U.D.D. The other recipes are automatically unlocked afterward.

![Crafting recipe to get started](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c4c279118f3edb9530889ce476aa4aa62ebab7c6.png)

- Pressing the "Swap offhand item" button (F by default) let's you alternate between the 2 -equipped nozzles.
- Sneaking while in a body of water let's you refill your F.L.U.D.D.
- Rick clicking while F.L.U.D.D is equipped let's you use the current equipped nozzle.
- Nozzle boxes are used to equip different nozzles. They are opened by jumping on them.

You can't hold any items in your offhand while a F.L.U.D.D is equipped and the only way to unequip it is to manually remove it in your inventory offhand slot.

Each F.L.U.D.D reservoir/equipped nozzles is player dependant. So don't bother carrying multiple F.L.U.D.Ds.

# Dislcaimer regarding datapack conflicts
I am not held accountable for any conflicts with other datapacks you have installed. It may be better to use this datapack standalone to avoid any problems or if you aren't sure of what you are doing.
